channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Overgrown tree, 17th June
item.description:
Overgrown tree Dangerously leaning Into garden fence of 29 Leslie Road n2 8BN , back fence faces park gate road N2 8BY property of block agent for Lorraine court n2 8by. Branches overgrown stabbing into fence damaging it ..and tree looks like dangerously also leaning towards fence. Directly opposite Lorraine Court Car park. I have written email to block management company Martyn Gerard. Awaiting response …but should they refuse to carry out maintenance on this tree I will need back up from council to enforce this.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 19, Percy Road, North Finchley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Household items and furniture on the pavement, 17th June
item.description:
Items left here almost every day. Usually things that could easily be recycled

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Alkham Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Gate to grass area, 17th June
item.description:
First seen this am The gate has been pulled off the wall with it's bracket, It was blocking pavement moved to aid access for those information or poor sight.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Shepherdess Walk, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Horrrible toxic smell for several weeks, 17th June
item.description:
Toxic smell in this area for over two weeks on and off. I can't find any road works, its of either like a burnt cable or burnt rubber? maybe its like a pavement smell? some weeks ago there was an electricity problem in this area which caused blackout
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Hartlake Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead fox in blackbag, 17th June
item.description:
Dead baby fox in black bag nxt to leb station opposite nursery entrance
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Flaxton Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish dumped in lake, 17th June
item.description:
Rubbish has been dumped from Bazalgette way into Southmere lake What3words location. punch.rival.home

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bazalgette Way, Lower Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pothole next to drop down curb where people cross the road., 17th June
item.description:
This was already reported (Report ref: 9553146) but was closed out without suitable investigation as the potholes exceeds the size and depth for action under Bexley Council policy but was closed off without a visit to review the size of the pothole. Please arrange repair before someone is badly injured.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 20, Upland Road, Barnehurst, Bexleyheath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter and sewage on pavement, 17th June
item.description:
Seems to be sewage flowing over pavement and into the road. Also lots of litter along the street

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Clarence Mews, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Glass smashed in shelter, 17th June
item.description:
The bus stop on Whitehall lane just before bridge road has a smashed panel and there is glass all over the floor, many kids walk this way for school and people with pushchairs. It is all over the path which can cause someone to get the glass stuck in their shoes/feet.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Whitehall Lane, North End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Damage - smashed glass or panels / jagged metal edges on shelter

item.title: Overgrown tree on Palmerston Rd, just before Spencer Ave. junction, 17th June
item.description:
This tree has been overgrown for at least 2 months. It is blocking part of the pavement. Pedestrians are having to walk into the into or onto private driveways in order to pass. on Palmerston Road, opposite the bike lockers, just before the Spencer Avenue street sign.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Palmerston Road, Southgate
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping for years, 17th June
item.description:
Dear [Council/Property Manager], I am writing to formally raise a long-standing concern regarding persistent fly-tipping by council residents into the Hyde-managed bins. This issue has been ongoing for several years, and despite previous reports, the situation has continued to escalate. Residents from neighbouring council properties are repeatedly disposing of household waste, bulky items, and general rubbish in bins that are designated exclusively for Hyde residents. As a result, the bins are frequently overflowing, leading to unhygienic conditions, increased vermin activity, and avoidable collection delays. This has created an unpleasant and unfair environment for those of us who follow the proper waste‑disposal rules. The problem has now reached a point where it is affecting the day‑to‑day living standards of Hyde residents. Many of us are unable to use our own bins because they are already filled with waste that does not belong to our block. This is not only inconvenient but also raises health and safety concerns. I kindly request that the council take immediate and effective action to address this issue. Potential measures could include: Increased monitoring or enforcement in the area Clear signage indicating that the bins are for Hyde residents only Education or communication to council residents about correct waste‑disposal procedures Installation of CCTV or other deterrents if appropriate Hyde residents should not be expected to manage or bear the consequences of waste produced by others. I hope this matter can be treated with the seriousness it deserves and that a sustainable solution can be put in place. I would appreciate a response outlining the steps that will be taken to resolve this ongoing issue.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 90, London Road, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Burnt out car debris, 17th June
item.description:
A burnt out car has been removed but this debris is still there.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 65, Leesons Way, St Paul's Cray, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped sofa, 17th June
item.description:
Sofa dumped on street

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Regular overflowing bin outside in2pappadam, 17th June
item.description:
The bin is regularly overflowing and does not appear to be emptied daily. This leads to regular fly tipping of bin bags and dog waste left on the pavement.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 100, Bromley Road, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 4 Overgrown Trees on Spencer Avenue, 17th June
item.description:
The trees have been overgrown for at least 2 months. They are blocking the pavements. People are having to duck under them in order to pass. Families with buggies are having to go onto the roads in order to pass. From Number 31/29 down to the top of the road.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Spencer Avenue, Southgate
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Potholes near lovel Avenue junction, 17th June
item.description:
There is a pothole around manhole outside no 26 on Anthony road , another one outside no 27

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lovel Avenue, East Wickham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dog waste bins overflowing, 17th June
item.description:
The 2 dog waste bins on the green are overflowing which is also leading to rubbish being left on top of them
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Albemarle Road, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Ash tree has overgrown., 17th June
item.description:
Ash tree has over grown into the number 13 back garden, the roots at about 2 meters away from the back of the house. has also started spouted out small branches coming out of the lawn.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 29, Collindale Avenue, Blackfen, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Deepening pothole, 17th June
item.description:
Getting worse daily.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ashfield Lane, Chislehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: This has just got a whole lot worse, 17th June
item.description:
Residents have left furniture outside on their property and it seems to have invited everyone to dump. They may be waiting someone to collect this for all I know? But now it’s blocking pavement access for all users who have to walk into the road to get past. I’ve picked up the rubbish and binned as much as I can.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Hylton Street, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet
